rulings.Case Title: 741 CASTRO LLC VS. DEAN BALLBACK et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON TUESDAY, MAY 22, 2007, LINE 4. CROSS DEFENDANT FIDELITY NATIONAL TITLE COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT IS CONTINUED TO JUNE 11, 2007 ON COURT'S MOTION. =(302/PJM/KD)

rulings.Case Title: NEIL ADAMS VS. CORT BUSINESS SERVICES CORPORATION et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON TUESDAY, MAY 22, 2007, LINE 11. DEFENDANTS CORT BUSINESS SERVICES CORPORATION, DAWN KLIPPEL AND KEVIN DANA'S DEMURRER TO THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT. DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED. HEARING REQUIRED ON WHETHER TO GRANT LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF DID EXHAUST HIS ADMINISTRATIVE REMEDIES BY FILING AN AMENDED D.F.E.H. COMPLAINT AND ISSUES RELATED TO KLIPPEL WERE ADDRESSED. THE AMENDED COMPLAINT FAILS TO CONNECT KLIPPEL'S ALLEGED STATEMENTS TO PLAINTIFF OR TO ALLEGE FACTS DEMONSTRATING PERVASIVE OR SEVERE CONDUCT. THE AMENDED COMPLAINT FAILS TO ALLEGE HOW A CO-WORKER DID ENGAGE IN RETALIATION. =(302/PJM/AP)

rulings.Case Title: NEW YORK CHOCOLATE & CONFECTIONS COMPANY, A VS. OUSMANNE GBANE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON TUESDAY, MAY 22, 2007, LINE 5. PLAINTIFF NEW YORK CHOCOLATE & CONFECTIONS COMPANY'S Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint IS GRANTED. NO OPPOSITION FILED. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: ALL BAY CITIES CONSTRUCTION, A CALIFORNIA VS. NANCY MATTINGLY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Expunge Lis Pendens And To Remove Mechanics' Lien;
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON TUESDAY, MAY 22, 2007, LINE 6. DEFENDANT NANCY MATTINGLY'S Motion To Expunge Lis Pendens And To Remove Mechanics' Lien. HEARING REQUIRED. - CONTRACTOR'S LIEN IS NOT BARRED BECAUSE THE OWNER DID NOT SIGN OFF ON THE NOTICE OF COMPLETION FILED IN JULY. (CIVIL CODE SECTION 3093) - NO "COST TO FIX" PROVIDED FOR ITEMS LISTED IN PARAGRAPH SEVEN OF MATTINGLY DECLARATION. - MATTINGLY DOES NOT ADDRESS THE "EXTRAS" IN EXHIBIT D. - NEITHER SIDE EFFECTIVELY ADDRESSES THE ISSUE OF "DELAY". - CONTRACTOR DOES NOT EXPLAIN WHY AN "UNDERTAKING" IS NOT AN ADEQUATE REMEDY. =(302/PJM/AP)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES W SLOATE VS. OFFICE OF REAL ESTATE APPRAISERS
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Hearing Of Writ For Administrative Mandate In Proceedings To Compel State Agency To Vacate Order Revoking License
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON TUESDAY, MAY 22, 2007, LINE 9. PETITIONER JAMES SLOATE'S Writ For Administrative Mandate In Proceedings To Compel State Agency To Vacate Order Revoking License IS DENIED. THE WEIGHT OF THE EVIDENCE SUPPORTS THE ADMINISTRATIVE DECISION OF LIABILITY AND THERE IS NOT A MANIFEST ABUSE OF DISCRETION WITH RESPECT TO THE PENALTY IMPOSED UNDER LANDAU V. SUPERIOR COURT (1998) 81 CAL. APP. 4TH 191, 221. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: PRIME APARTMENT PROPERTIES IV LLC VS. JOHNNY VANHORN et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON TUESDAY, MAY 22, 2007, LINE 10. DEFENDANT JOHNNY VANHORN'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. NOTICES TO QUIT ARE STRICTLY ENFORCED AND PLAINTIFF'S NOTICE REFERS TO THE YEAR AS "2031". =(302/PJM/PB)
